title: "Privacy Law Roundup — April 28, 2026"
date: 2026-04-28 11:13:58 +0530
categories: [news, roundup]
tags: [dpdpa, india, privacy, global, news]
---

## India: DPDPA Legal Challenges

The Supreme Court has issued notices to the government regarding multiple petitions challenging the constitutionality of India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act, 2023, and the Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025. One significant concern raised is the Act's potential impact on the Right to Information by diluting certain provisions. This signals ongoing judicial scrutiny as the new privacy framework takes full effect.
([Source](https://news.google.)) ([Source](https://news.google.)) ([Source](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMioAFBVV95cUxNUXo3aUxwM1dVSWIzOUg4dzgtSkEtS210VFhhTkVIeS1KSHU4cHI0Vkd1enlUd0JKTmxHbTlpbDJyWVhPQmwzRWlpZnNXemZUcXRnOG51X0RYbGhYMElfejVJa2t4ZXhidnJEUkJaZVFLam50RkJubWo3VTJ2Q0YwSXAxVHk1UlhBSUsyVjdudk9RTEFraFhXT0M4UU5KNzZE))

## India: DPDPA Implementation and Compliance

With the DPDPA and its associated 2025 rules now fully in force, businesses are actively navigating the operational impacts, focusing on defining data processing scope, ensuring lawful data handling, and preparing for enforcement by the Data Protection Board. Various compliance guides are being released to assist organizations in understanding their new obligations and the fundamentals of the Act.
([Source](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiekFVX3lxTFA0MUhxR2JRbkZEUG81Smp1Uy02Z0xtdWZYSnlyLVJVSHQ2eWtleENVY2Iya2tVTUNveFE5Mm1FLUVwd01icDNwT0pqcFZyOGJVNmZSRGJoSjJUT21BOWxkOXFqTmhVRmNSVW5jSGFyQkVlblRoR2FTX01R)) ([Source](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMipAFBVV95cUxOcXF6RDJyNU14a2FBNktObEt6VnhKeEI1LUNOMlROM1RkSFpZMmFWU3NzQXRzWUk3OGU1bXowTHNOUjNkZEk1eXoxMTF1SVJiMDlGMHNqRzN6OFB4ejY1akRXaDdpRDVqSTVXMFBNbklZZzI0ekJhWE1FbjNZSmxQZkVQbWhBYmpRY1lPN3dBMFBlOUphVVMxZHRHMThGN3I1a2FrMg)) ([Source](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMixwFBVV95cUxNZG5MSDhBRmpzczRjbkh2b1F3SFljbGl3VlEzMkFnYnZkNTJ4VTI4bkpuZmtSV3lqbTRiV3d6YWFmdnJTVS1sVklTcGM2U2NzQXBIV0pQWU9UZEYxQW15VEE5SWpLNGlfVXdqbWR3QzFaVU9lVXkxeHU3dGxYRTZRWGU1djh3c3VBQkx3WnduTlZpaElqM1ZYYzNtTEdJOGpybEZkbS1XZElzMWR6Z3BKd1hZcTFmNXl4b1d6VmRXd3hyWmxzOXB3)) ([Source](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiggFBVV95cUxNRGl2X2FCalZyMnExLTlfN01QVF82SVFNUXZ1azRJdDZUd0hyNjE1YVNjc0hKUGYwZEVqRzVUaXk3VDh0MUVfb1pVS0x0SnlBdDNKWmIxOHh3QzVQUktkMEJpLWdqdVBqSDl5MXpqbC1MbFA5Um1qbHVSOVM2VmJqeGxn)) ([Source](https://news.google.com/rss/articles/CBMiggFBVV95cUxQVFgzNmxORS1FMnFOdVBLYVIyTmR4MjlGd1NuZHp0aUowX3hKajA4eWJTQ2ptbEdvM0JtdjloRU1qY3liUmdiUXFpMnV3OUhFbTYxZnRMVm1RVVJCalI0MVhaZW1nSEQzdnREYU0xbHZ0QlZCMWxvUGpMc0dCNlhMMFhB))
Technology providers are rapidly introducing solutions to help Indian enterprises achieve DPDPA compliance, particularly focusing on data sovereignty and secure data management. Several companies have launched new services and partnerships aimed at simplifying the complex requirements for businesses.
